votes.reason: The Python code correctly translates the Coq specification, using the same lists, list indexing with default values, and arithmetic operations to construct and compare the Roman numeral string.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. The numeral lists are identical, nth_default behaves the same way (returning the element at index n if valid, otherwise the default), and the arithmetic operations (division, modulo) produce the same indices. The string concatenation produces the same result. The precondition check (1 <= number <= 1000) matches the Coq Prop constraint, and the final comparison checks if result equals the expected concatenation.
